commit.message:
Fix interleave: replace broken single-pass swap with two-bucket merge

The old interleave() walked left-to-right and swapped the 3rd consecutive
same-article word with the nearest different one ahead. This produced a
predictable 'de de het de de het' repeating pattern instead of random
distribution.

New approach:
- Split words into separate de/het buckets, shuffle each independently
- Merge with a weighted coin flip (proportional to remaining bucket sizes)
- Hard constraint: max 2 consecutive same-article words
- Graceful fallback when one bucket runs out (unavoidable with 270 de vs 188 het)

Tested: 100 trials of full array, max run = 2 for first ~376 words.
Only 'All words' mode can exceed 2 at the tail when het words are exhausted.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix provider display, gateway activation, and setup re-entry bugs

Key issues fixed:
- FutureBuilder anti-pattern: GatewayControls was creating a new
  readConfig() future on every rebuild, causing brief empty-state
  flashes that disabled the Start Gateway button and showed 'no
  providers' even when providers were saved. Replaced with cached
  _config state loaded in initState.
- No reactive updates: Added ProviderConfigService.configChangedListenable
  (ValueNotifier<int>) that fires after every write. GatewayControls and
  ProvidersScreen both listen and refresh automatically — covers the
  IndexedStack case where the Providers tab was stale after edits.
- Setup re-entry: Splash now checks for a configured provider after
  bootstrap completes. If binary exists but no provider saved (e.g. app
  closed during onboarding), routes to OnboardingScreen(isFirstRun:true)
  instead of Dashboard.
- 'Configure Provider' opened blank OnboardingScreen: Changed
  _openProviderSetup in GatewayControls to push ProvidersScreen instead,
  so users see existing providers and can add/edit — no more blank form.
- Removed stale setState(() {}) after setActiveProviderConfig since the
  change notifier now triggers _loadConfig automatically.

* Fix Wolverine shutdown exception filter to handle nested AggregateException

The existing catch clause checked only direct InnerExceptions, so it missed
cases where Wolverine wrapped the ObjectDisposedException(EventLogInternal)
inside another AggregateException during DrainAsync.

The shutdown sequence is:
  1. AMQP link drain exceeds 3-second budget  TimeoutException (logged by ListeningAgent)
  2. Wolverine calls LogError() to record the timeout  EventLogInternal is
     already disposed  ThrowLoggingError() raises AggregateException(ObjectDisposedException)
  3. Wolverine's DrainAsync wraps that in a second AggregateException before
     re-throwing, so the filter never matched.

Fix: call Flatten() before the All() check, and add TimeoutException to the
allowed set so both leaf exception types from the inline-listener teardown
path are suppressed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix handler test to verify PublishAsync instead of ScheduleAsync extension method

ScheduleAsync<T>(msg, TimeSpan) is a static extension method in Wolverine's
MessageBusExtensions  Moq cannot set up or verify extension methods because
they are not virtual interface members.

The extension method delegates directly to IMessageBus.PublishAsync<T>(msg, options)
with options.ScheduleDelay set to the requested delay, so the correct thing
to verify is PublishAsync with a DeliveryOptions matcher.

ci(tooling): Generate release notes via Copilot CLI

The release workflow was calling the GitHub Models REST API directly to
generate polished release notes. This approach was brittle: it required
a dedicated PAT with Copilot entitlements, the API returned 403 on
accounts without the right plan, and there was no structured fallback
beyond a minimal bullet list — meaning any failure silently degraded
the release page quality.

The new approach has three stages:

1. A deterministic Node script (scripts/generate-release-notes.mjs)
   always runs first. It parses the git log and the PR metadata already
   collected by the workflow's github-script step, and produces a fully
   structured Markdown draft following the exact changelog format
   (prefix symbols, sections, contributors) but with a placeholder
   comment where the LLM summary paragraph should go. This draft is
   both the input to stage 2 and the high-quality fallback for stage 3.

2. The Copilot CLI (copilot -p ... --no-ask-user) is installed on the
   runner and asked to replace the placeholder with a polished 2-4
   sentence summary and write the result to release-notes.md. It reads
   the SKILL instructions, the commit context, and the deterministic
   draft from the filesystem — no external API call or secret beyond
   the COPILOT_GITHUB_TOKEN PAT is needed.

3. If the CLI exits non-zero or does not write the output file, the
   deterministic draft is used as-is. This guarantees that every release
   always gets correctly formatted notes regardless of LLM availability.

The release-notes prompt has been migrated from the ad-hoc workflow
asset (.github/workflows/assets/release-notes-prompt.md) to a SKILL
(.agents/skills/release-notes/SKILL.md) so it can be discovered and
reused by the Copilot CLI and any future automation.

A vitest test suite (scripts/generate-release-notes.spec.mjs, 41 tests)
covers all deterministic generation logic: commit parsing, type-to-prefix
mapping, conventional subject parsing, backtick escaping, PR link
rendering, contributor deduplication, and edge cases (no commits,
non-conventional subjects).

commit.message:
perf(analytics): O(1) incremental OLS update + forecasting v1.9.0 acceptance criteria (#4613)

* Initial plan

* perf(analytics): O(1) incremental OLS update for LINEAR_REGRESSION in update()

Add running OLS moment sums (lin_sx, lin_sy, lin_sxx, lin_sxy, lin_n) to
ForecastModel::Impl.  fitLinear() initializes them in O(n) once during fit();
subsequent update() calls update them in O(1) and recompute alpha/beta
analytically.  Residual std-dev is tracked via an EMA for O(1) CI updates.

Cache restore path also restores running sums so the first update() after
a cache-hit fit() is O(1).  A graceful fallback to full O(n) refit is
provided for models restored via deserialize() (which does not persist
running sums).

Add two new tests:
- Update_LinearRegression_IncrementalOLS: single update matches full refit
- Update_LinearRegression_MultipleUpdatesConsistent: 5 updates match refit

* recipe parser: handle list-of-objects in capability config values

Enhance the YAML recipe parser to support structured config entries like:
  allow:
    - proto: tcp
      ports: [80, 443]

Each object in the list is flattened to a "key=value;key=value" string
that is stored in the existing config values list. This preserves the
human-friendly YAML format while keeping the binary serialization and
runtime deserialization unchanged.

Also update cvd_client __parse_network_rule to parse the generic
"proto=tcp;ports=80,443" flattened format, and revert docs to show the
structured YAML syntax.
